---
title: "Cognitive–Motor Interference Under Load"
subtitle: "Complete Portfolio Case Study — BAP Gripforce × Perceptual Decision-Making"
author: "Mohd Dastgheib"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "View code"
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 9
    fig-dpi: 144
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(gt)
})

repo <- normalizePath("..", winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)
if (!file.exists(file.path(repo, "reports", "analysis_trial_table.csv"))) {
  repo <- normalizePath(".", winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)
}

fig_dir <- file.path(repo, "reports", "figures")
model_path <- file.path(repo, "reports", "analysis_trial_table.csv")
fx_path <- file.path(repo, "reports", "cognitive_motor_glmer_fixed_effects.csv")
attrition_path <- file.path(repo, "reports", "grip_attrition_funnel.csv")

stopifnot(
  file.exists(model_path),
  file.exists(fx_path)
)

model_tbl <- read.csv(model_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
fx_tbl <- read.csv(fx_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
attrition <- read.csv(attrition_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

n_trials <- nrow(model_tbl)
n_subjects <- length(unique(model_tbl$subject_id))
force_r <- cor(
  model_tbl$grip_force_mean,
  model_tbl$grip_force_mean_au,
  use = "complete.obs"
)

fig_files <- c(
  "hero_single_trial_anatomy.png",
  "hero_probe_locked_spectrogram.png",
  "hero_spectral_fingerprint.png",
  "hero_spectral_feature_space.png",
  "hero_tremor_accuracy_lift.png",
  "hero_grip_compliance.png",
  "hero_psychometric_by_grip.png",
  "hero_direction_flip.png",
  "hero_psd_ribbon.png",
  "hero_caterpillar.png",
  "hero_sat_tradeoff.png"
)
fig_paths <- file.path(fig_dir, fig_files)
missing_figs <- fig_paths[!file.exists(fig_paths)]
if (length(missing_figs) > 0) {
  stop(
    "Missing figures. Run `quarto render analysis/03_hero_visuals.qmd` first:\n",
    paste(missing_figs, collapse = "\n")
  )
}
```

## LLM / reviewer brief {#llm-brief}

**One-sentence pitch:** Continuous 100 Hz grip-force streams carry trial-level spectral signatures of cognitive–motor interference that predict perceptual oddball accuracy beyond nominal effort labels.

**Dataset:** Older-adult BAP cohort · auditory + visual oddball · low/high grip effort · `r n_trials` analysis trials · `r n_subjects` subjects.

**Pipeline:** Target-normalized force error → linear detrend → zero-phase 0.5 Hz high-pass (`sosfiltfilt`) → Welch PSD → band power (0.5–3 Hz tracking, 8–12 Hz tremor) → mixed-effects logistic regression of `resp_is_correct`.

**Primary analysis:** High grip only (40% MVC). Low grip (5% MVC) is a comparison arm — pooled models are misleading because tremor effects flip sign across grip conditions.

**Headline results:**

- Force QA: recomputed trial means vs behavioral v3, **r = `r sprintf('%.3f', force_r)`**.
- High-grip compliance: median 1.09× target (40% MVC held with high fidelity across 44 subjects).
- **Direction flip:** tremor OR < 1 under high grip (interference prediction); OR > 1 under low grip (precision-noise artifact of near-zero target).
- Psychometric function: physical load compresses P("Different") at intermediate signal levels (Δ2).

**Ask for feedback on:** (1) scientific narrative, (2) figure clarity for quant-UX portfolio, (3) whether probe-locked spectrogram supports the “cognitive shock” claim, (4) modeling choices.

---

## 1. Scientific framing

### Research question

Does the moment-to-moment quality of concurrent grip-force control during perceptual decision-making predict accuracy beyond task modality, stimulus difficulty, and grip effort condition?

### Hypothesis

The motor system acts as a high-fidelity mirror of cognitive processing: high-frequency instability (8–12 Hz tremor band) and low-frequency tracking error (0.5–3 Hz) during dual-task performance should explain perceptual errors, especially under physical load.

### Why spectral features?

Prior work treats grip as a binary effort covariate. We treat the 100 Hz stream as a **continuous behavioral phenotype**, extracting physics-informed features that survive target normalization (same arbitrary units as behavioral v3 `grip_force_mean_au`).

---

## 2. Cohort & attrition

```{r}
#| label: attrition-table

attrition %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Grip analysis attrition funnel",
    subtitle = "Trial counts at each stage; % of retainable behavioral trials in final column"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    stage = "Stage",
    n_subject_tasks = "Subject-tasks",
    n_trials = "Trials",
    pct_of_retainable_beh = "% retainable beh",
    note = "Note"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = pct_of_retainable_beh, decimals = 1, pattern = "{x}%") %>%
  sub_missing(columns = pct_of_retainable_beh, missing_text = "—") %>%
  tab_options(table.font.size = px(13))
```

**Analysis set:** `r format(n_trials, big.mark = ",")` trials in behavioral v3 · **`r format(sum(attrition$n_trials[attrition$stage == "Analysis set (trial features extracted)"]), big.mark = ",")` trials with grip features** (`r sprintf("%.0f%%", attrition$pct_of_retainable_beh[attrition$stage == "Analysis set (trial features extracted)"])` of retainable behavioral) · `r n_subjects` subjects · aud + vis oddball only.

---

## 3. DSP pipeline

Per trial, per 100 Hz sample:

$$
e(t) = \frac{F(t) - F_{\text{target}}}{F_{\text{target}}}, \quad F_{\text{target}} = \text{grip\_targ\_prop\_mvc} \times \text{mvc}
$$

1. **RMS error** on raw $e(t)$
2. **Linear detrend** → **4th-order Butterworth high-pass @ 0.5 Hz** (`scipy.signal.sosfiltfilt`, zero-phase)
3. **Welch PSD** → integrate **0.5–3 Hz** (tracking) and **8–12 Hz** (tremor)

**Probe timing note:** `stim_offset` is **seconds** for visual trials (probe onset within epoch) but **Hz frequency shift** for auditory trials. Derived column `probe_onset_s` = `stim_offset` (vis) or 0 (aud).

---

## 4. Hero visualizations

### 4.1 Signal chain — time domain + Welch PSD

```{r}
#| label: fig-anatomy
#| fig-cap: "Single-trial signal chain: force, normalized error, rolling instability, and Welch PSD with DSP bands."

knitr::include_graphics(fig_paths[1])
```

### 4.2 Probe-locked spectrogram (cognitive shock)

```{r}
#| label: fig-specgram
#| fig-cap: "Probe-locked spectrogram: time × frequency power aligned to oddball onset (t = 0)."

knitr::include_graphics(fig_paths[2])
```

### 4.3 Population spectral fingerprint

```{r}
#| label: fig-fingerprint
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of log10 band power by grip effort and perceptual outcome."

knitr::include_graphics(fig_paths[3])
```

### 4.4 Two-band feature space

```{r}
#| label: fig-feature-space
#| fig-cap: "Tracking vs tremor band power; each point is one trial."

knitr::include_graphics(fig_paths[4])
```

### 4.5 Behavioral lift — tremor × effort

```{r}
#| label: fig-lift
#| fig-cap: "Model-predicted accuracy as a function of tremor-band power, split by grip effort."

knitr::include_graphics(fig_paths[5])
```

### 4.6 Grip compliance (40% MVC)

```{r}
#| label: fig-compliance
#| fig-cap: "Per-subject force compliance confirms high-grip spectral features are well-calibrated."

knitr::include_graphics(fig_paths[6])
```

### 4.7 Psychometric function under load

```{r}
#| label: fig-psychometric
#| fig-cap: "P('Different') rises monotonically with signal strength; high grip compresses the function at intermediate levels."

knitr::include_graphics(fig_paths[7])
```

### 4.8 Direction flip — tremor under load

```{r}
#| label: fig-flip
#| fig-cap: "Opposite tremor slopes and ORs for high vs low grip — the key sanity check."

knitr::include_graphics(fig_paths[8])
```

---

## 5. Mixed-effects modeling

**Primary cohort:** high grip (40% MVC) only. Trial-level logistic GLMMs with random intercepts for `subject_id`:

- **Baseline:** `resp_is_correct ~ task_modality + stim_level_index + (1 | subject_id)`
- **DSP-augmented:** adds z-scored `pow_05_3hz` and `pow_8_12hz`

Low-grip trials are fitted identically as a comparison arm (see stratified tremor table in `02_cognitive_motor_model.qmd`).

```{r}
#| label: fx-table

fx_tbl %>%
  rename(`OR (95% CI)` = OR..95..CI.) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "Model") %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Fixed effects — high grip (40% MVC)",
    subtitle = "Odds ratios (95% CI) from mixed-effects logistic regression"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    Predictor = "Predictor",
    `OR (95% CI)` = "OR (95% CI)",
    p = "p"
  ) %>%
  tab_options(table.font.size = px(12))
```

**Interpretation:** Under physical load (40% MVC), tremor-band power trends negative (more tremor → lower accuracy). The pooled model that included both grip conditions was misleading — the significant positive tremor effect was driven by low-grip precision noise, not load-induced interference.

---

## 6. Feature summaries

```{r}
#| label: feature-summary

model_tbl %>%
  filter(!is.na(pow_05_3hz), !is.na(pow_8_12hz)) %>%
  summarise(
    trials = n(),
    `rms_error (mean)` = mean(rms_error, na.rm = TRUE),
    `pow_0.5-3Hz (median)` = median(pow_05_3hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    `pow_8-12Hz (median)` = median(pow_8_12hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    accuracy = mean(tolower(as.character(resp_is_correct)) == "true", na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(`rms_error (mean)`, `pow_0.5-3Hz (median)`, `pow_8-12Hz (median)`), decimals = 4) %>%
  fmt_percent(columns = accuracy, decimals = 1) %>%
  tab_header(title = "Analysis-set feature summaries")
```

---

## 7. Additional Analyses

### 7a. Population Mean PSD

```{r}
#| label: fig-psd-ribbon
#| fig-cap: "Grand-average Welch PSD ± SE by grip condition. Low grip (5% MVC) shows dramatically elevated tremor-band power (8–12 Hz), confirming inadequate motor control under a near-zero target. High grip (40% MVC) suppresses the tremor band."

knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "hero_psd_ribbon.png"))
```

### 7b. Individual Differences: Load Cost Caterpillar

```{r}
#| label: fig-caterpillar
#| fig-cap: "Subject-level accuracy drop under high grip. ~70% of subjects show a detectable load cost; ~30% are facilitated, consistent with inverted-U arousal theory."

knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "hero_caterpillar.png"))
```

### 7c. Speed–Accuracy Trade-off Under Load

```{r}
#| label: fig-sat
#| fig-cap: "High grip shifts the SAT frontier downward (lower accuracy) without systematically changing response time, ruling out a pure speed–accuracy swap."

knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "hero_sat_tradeoff.png"))
```

### 7d. LC Brain–Behavior Relationship

The BAP dataset includes MRI-derived Locus Coeruleus integrity metrics (Bennett et al., *Sci. Rep.* 2024) for **N = 55** subjects. LC fICVF (NODDI restricted diffusion) shows a modest but consistent negative correlation with load-induced tremor increase (r ≈ −0.19): subjects with lower LC microstructural integrity show *greater* tremor reactivity under 40% MVC load. This is consistent with the LC-NE hypothesis that noradrenergic modulation supports motor regulation under cognitive demand.

*Full scatterplots in `02_cognitive_motor_model.qmd`.*

### 7e. Out-of-Sample ML Predictability

GroupKFold (leave-one-subject-out) cross-validation on high-grip trials (*N* = 65 subjects):

| Feature set | Model | AUC |
|-------------|-------|-----|
| Difficulty only | Random Forest | 0.850 |
| + DSP (rms, tracking, tremor) | Random Forest | **0.855** |
| Difficulty only | Logistic | 0.617 |
| + DSP | Logistic | **0.628** |

Task difficulty (`stim_level_index`) dominates. Grip-force DSP features add ~0.5% AUC — a real but modest independent contribution. The incremental lift is scientifically informative: grip instability carries signal about perceptual outcome *beyond* the nominal task design.

---

## 8. Limitations & next steps

1. **Pre/post-probe windows** — windowed feature extraction (pre: −1.5 to −0.1 s; post: +0.1 to +1.5 s relative to probe onset) is implemented and computed for visual trials (n = 1,444 with valid windows). Integration into the primary model is a next step.
2. **File attrition (post re-download)** — **78.1%** of retainable behavioral trials reach the analysis set (13,759 / 17,621). Residual loss: **0.7%** no grip files (BAP172 aud only), **20.9%** partial grip coverage, **3.8%** feature extraction.
3. **Grip stratification required** — pooled tremor effects flip sign across grip conditions; primary inference is high-grip only.
4. **Auditory probe timing** — `probe_onset_s = 0` for aud; visual trials have sub-second probe offsets.
5. **LC sample size** — LC × behavioral analysis is limited to n ≈ 55 subjects; larger samples needed to confirm r ≈ −0.19 for fICVF.

**Reproducibility:** Source modules — `01_ingest.qmd` (Python DSP), `02_cognitive_motor_model.qmd` (R GLMM), `03_hero_visuals.qmd` (R figures).

---

*Generated as a single self-contained HTML for portfolio review.*

**Export one file for LLM upload:**

```bash
quarto render analysis/portfolio.qmd
cp _site/analysis/portfolio.html portfolio_case_study.html
```

Open `portfolio_case_study.html` at the repo root — all figures are embedded (no external dependencies).
